title: Robots: Our Smart Helpers
passage:
Robots in Our Lives

Robots are very smart machines. Today, we can find robots in many different places. They help people at home, in schools, in offices, and in hospitals. Some robots are very small, and other robots are very big. They make our lives easier and safer.

In many modern homes, people use small robot vacuum cleaners. These robots move around the room and clean the floor. They have sensors, so they do not hit chairs or tables. They can clean when people are asleep or at work. This helps people save a lot of time every day.

In big factories, robots do difficult and dangerous work. For example, they can lift very heavy boxes and put them on trucks. They can also work for a long time without getting tired. They do not need to sleep, eat, or take a break. This helps companies make cars and electronics very fast and cheap.

In some schools, teachers use robots to help young children learn. These robots can speak different languages, read stories, and play educational games. Children love learning with robots because they think robots are like toys. In hospitals, robots also help doctors and nurses. They can carry medicine to patients or clean hospital rooms to keep them safe from germs.

In the future, we will see many more robots. Scientists are making smart robots that can talk like humans. These are called AI robots. AI means Artificial Intelligence. AI helps robots think, learn, and make decisions. Soon, robots might drive cars or help old people in their homes. Robots are changing our world every day.

questions.text: Where can we find robots today?
questions.explanation: The passage states: 'Today, we can find robots in many different places.'

options.TRUE: If the statement agrees with the information

options.NOT GIVEN: If there is no information on this

vocabulary.meaning: a tool that helps a machine find or feel things
a building where people make things using machines
very small living things that can make people sick
the power of machines to think and learn like humans

tactics:
Hướng dẫn làm bài (Tactics)

1. Kỹ năng tìm từ khóa (Keywords): Với trình độ A1-A2, các câu hỏi sử dụng từ khóa giống hệt trong bài đọc. Hãy khoanh tròn các từ quan trọng trong câu hỏi (ví dụ: 'sensors', 'factories', 'languages') rồi tìm chúng trong bài đọc.

2. Điền từ vào chỗ trống: Đọc kỹ giới hạn số từ (NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS). Bạn chỉ được lấy từ trực tiếp trong bài đọc để điền vào chỗ trống mà không cần thay đổi dạng của từ.

3. True/False/Not Given: Nếu thông tin trong câu hỏi hoàn toàn trái ngược với bài đọc, chọn FALSE. Nếu bài đọc không hề nhắc tới thông tin đó (ví dụ như giá tiền của robot), chọn NOT GIVEN.
